svg.font-family: Comic Sans MS, Chalkboard, cursive, sans-serif
svg.text: How bad guys can hide stolen data inside normal internet traffic

g.text: QUIC is how your phone talks to websites — FAST and SAFE!
Used by Google, YouTube, Facebook, and 30% of all web traffic!
Chapter 2: The Magic Name Tag (Connection ID)
If ANY of these change = LOST! 😭
"I know you by your NAME TAG!"
IP can change, port can change...
TCP = "The kid in seat 3, row 2" — if they move seats, teacher is confused!
QUIC = "The kid named Alex" — doesn't matter where they sit!
🤝 But first — a secret handshake! (Path Validation)
This prevents strangers from pretending to be you!
✨ Your YouTube video keeps playing — no buffering! ✨
Chapter 4: The Server Can Move Too!
Servers can say: "Hey, talk to me at this BETTER address instead!"
🔑 Key point: This "preferred address" is sent ENCRYPTED
Firewalls and middleboxes CANNOT see it — only the phone knows the new address!
A bad guy puts spy software on your computer. Here's what it does...
📝 "Hmm, I see your normal packets look like this..."
The spy pretends your PC is doing a normal server migration — but sends YOUR data to the bad guy!
It copies the SIZE, TIMING, and STYLE of real packets — so it looks 100% normal! 🎭
⚠️ 5 AI detectors tried to catch it — ALL FAILED!
Best detection score: only 0.35 out of 1.0 (F1-Score)
Chapter 6: Why Can't We Stop It? 🤔
2. Make the "preferred address" visible to firewalls
4. Check destination IPs against known servers
